stem: The function f(t) = 25,000·2t/520 gives the number of bacteria in a population t minutes after an initial observation. How much time, in minutes, does it take for the number of bacteria in the population to double?
rationale: Choice D is correct. The expression 2t/520 doubles when the exponent t/520 increases by 1, i.e., when t increases by 520. So the doubling time is 520 minutes. Choices A, B, and C come from misreading the base of the exponent or the initial value.
